channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Why I stayed at 'struggling' Man Utd and didn't think we'd be third now - Fernandes
item.description: Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes speaks to club legend Wayne Rooney in a wide-ranging interview.

item.title: Prestianni gets six-match ban for homophobic conduct
item.description: Gianluca Prestianni is given a six-game ban by Uefa for homophobic conduct during Benfica's Champions League knockout play-off match with Real Madrid in February.

item.title: The remarkable rise of Chelsea's little-known interim boss
item.description: Chelsea's interim head coach Calum McFarlane admits this year has been a "whirlwind", going from obscurity to an FA Cup semi‑final at Wembley.

item.title: Who will win the title? Reporters, fans and pundits make their predictions
item.description: Will it be Manchester City or Arsenal for the Premier League title? BBC Sport gets pundits, reporters and fans to predict each of their remaining fixtures - and who will win the league.

item.title: West Brom docked two points for financial breaches
item.description: West Bromwich Albion are given a two-point penalty by the English Football League for breaching profit and sustainability rules.

item.title: How Lineker and Neville became rival media moguls
item.description: BBC sports editor Dan Roan examines how Gary Lineker and Gary Neville became two of the biggest players in a rapidly changing media industry.

item.title: Why Wembley is special, for FA Cup semi-finals too
item.description: Tony Pulis explains why getting to Wembley is not just special for any manager, it is still hugely important for players, fans and all the staff at any club.

item.title: Hearts, Rangers, Celtic - who will lead Premiership by end of Sunday?
item.description: Three teams separated by three points with just five games to go. After an epic season, the Scottish Premiership split is finally here.

item.title: Sutton's predictions v Race Across the World podcast's Alfie Watts
item.description: Chris Sutton takes on Race Across the World podcast travel expert Alfie Watts plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's fixtures.

item.title: Brobbey should have been sent off against Spurs - panel
item.description: Sunderland's Brian Brobbey should have been sent off for two yellow-card offences against Tottenham, a Premier League panel has ruled.

item.title: Rochdale and York set for 'unique' title decider
item.description: York City boss Stuart Maynard calls his side's final day National League title decider at Rochdale "one of the biggest games in the English football pyramid.

item.title: Ex-Brighton boss Hughton reveals cancer diagnosis
item.description: Former Brighton and Newcastle manager Chris Hughton reveals he was treated for prostate cancer last year.

item.title: Former Nigeria striker Eneramo dies during match
item.description: Michael Eneramo, who won 10 caps for the Super Eagles, dies aged 40 after suffering a suspected cardiac arrest during a friendly game.

item.title: Five things in the EFL: penultimate peril and jittery jeopardy
item.description: Here are five things to look out for across the EFL in this penultimate weekend of the regular season.

item.title: Staying this long at Man Utd isn't for everyone - Fernandes
item.description: Bruno Fernandes opens up about his time at Manchester United and why he decided to stay at the club.

item.title: 'I try to stay happy' - Trafford on tricky start to life at Man City
item.description: Manchester City goalkeeper James Trafford says he has not had "the best possible outcome" since joining the club.

item.title: Man City move top with win as Burnley relegation confirmed
item.description: Manchester City continue their charge towards the Premier League title with victory at Turf Moor to move Pep Guardiola's side top of the table and relegate Burnley back to the Championship.

item.title: Leeds score late to earn draw at Bournemouth
item.description: Sean Longstaff scores a dramatic 97th-minute equaliser as Leeds took another step towards Premier League survival in an eventful draw at Bournemouth.

item.title: Man City focusing on winning 'all five games' in title run-in - Guardiola
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says his side is focusing on winning "all fives games" during the title run-in, after their 1-0 win against Burnley in the Premier League.

item.title: Rangers fans face ban for derby amid dispute with Celtic
item.description: Rangers ask the Scottish Professional Football League to step in after Celtic refused to give away fans any tickets to the final Old Firm derby of the season unless the Ibrox club's Union Bears ultras group is banned.

item.title: What next in battle to avoid Premiership play-off?
item.description: Livingston could go down as soon as this weekend, but how is the battle to avoid the Premiership relegation play-off shaping up?

item.title: Millwall consider legal action over badge on KKK image
item.description: Millwall consider taking legal action after Westminster City Council used the club's badge on an illustration of a Ku Klux Klan member.

item.title: Artist to sue Sunderland over away kit design
item.description: An artist is planning legal action against Sunderland, claiming they reproduced an image of one of his sculptures on their away kit without his permission.

item.title: Man Utd hopeful of Toone return at Spurs
item.description: Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone will train on Friday and could return after four months out with injury at Tottenham on Sunday.

item.title: US rights groups urge caution for World Cup visitors
item.description: More than 120 organisations issue a travel advisory for visitors to this summer's World Cup, warning of potential threats to their human rights.

item.title: Iraola frustrated after Leeds' equaliser was not ruled out for offside
item.description: Bournemouth boss Andoni Iraola is frustrated with the decision to not rule out Sean Longstaff's 97th-minute equaliser for Leeds as a player was "offside in front of his goalkeeper".

item.title: Farke delighted with hard-fought point at Bournemouth
item.description: Leeds United manager Daniel Farke is full of praise for the "mentality" of his side as Sean Longstaff's 97th-minute goal earned his side a "hard-fought" point away at Bournemouth.

item.title: Brighton beat Chelsea in Rosenior's final match in charge
item.description: Brighton ease to a comfortable 3-0 win over Chelsea thanks to goals from Ferdi Kadioglu, Jack Hinshelwood and Danny Welbeck.

item.title: Rosenior's last post-match interview as Chelsea head coach
item.description: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior says his side's 3-0 defeat away at Brighton was "unacceptable" from everyone involved and that things need to change.

item.title: Burnley must come back stronger after 'relentless' season - Parker
item.description: Burnley head coach Scott Parker says his side must "come back stronger" after their 1-0 defeat to Manchester City meant they were relegated from the Premier League.

item.title: Howe 'looks forward' to talks with Newcastle owners
item.description: Head coach Eddie Howe says he is looking forward to sitting down with Newcastle United's owners next week to "express things from my perspective".

item.title: Gibbs-White's month to prove World Cup worth
item.description: Morgan Gibbs-White has hit form at the right time with Nottingham Forest - but did not make Thomas Tuchel's 35-man England squad last month. If he is to make it to the World Cup, time is tight.

item.title: Season has been big learning experience - Trafford
item.description: Goalkeeper James Trafford says this season has been a "big learning experience" and his move to Manchester City "hasn't been the best possible outcome".

item.title: Any Celtic boss would back new deals for trio - O'Neill
item.description: Interim manager Martin O'Neill thinks whoever takes permanent control this summer would be happy that Liam Scales, Luke McCowan and James Forrest have been handed new deals.

item.title: Why Wrexham's global fanbase is about more than fame and glory
item.description: They have famous owners and have been successful on and off the pitch, but what else is attracting football fans worldwide to Wrexham?

item.title: Big clubs and big names - a summer of managerial upheaval awaits
item.description: BBC Sport senior football correspondent Sami Mokbel analyses a summer that promises plenty of change in the Premier League.

item.title: Retiring kit woman honoured with giant tifo banner
item.description: Go Ahead Eagles reveal a huge tifo for retiring kit woman Carla Whittie who has been at the club for 28 years.

item.title: 'People can say whatever stupid things they want' - Guardiola on celebrations
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says his players can "celebrate however you want" in response to criticism from some pundits for his side's reaction to beating Arsenal on Sunday.

item.title: Watch the goals and celebrations as St Johnstone clinch title
item.description: St Johnstone win 2-0 at Dunfermline Athletic to wrap up the Scottish Championship title with two games to spare.

item.title: Can Arsenal's season be compared to McIlroy's 2025 Masters?
item.description: The Monday Night Club debate whether Arsenal's season is comparable to McIlroy's 2025 Masters.

item.title: West Ham earn valuable point at London rivals Crystal Palace
item.description: West Ham edge a point clearer of the relegation zone as they share a goalless draw with Crystal Palace at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Who is Iraola successor Rose?
item.description: German football journalist Constantin Eckner provides the background on Bournemouth's incoming head coach Marco Rose.

item.title: 'It will go all the way' - Nuno on relegation battle after draw at Palace
item.description: West Ham manager Nuno Espirito Santo belives the Premier League relegation battle "will go all the way" after his side's 0-0 draw with Crystal Palace at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Palace not clinical enough in West Ham draw - Glasner
item.description: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner says his side were "not clinical enough" in their 0-0 Premier League draw with West Ham at Selhurst Park.

item.title: How to follow the FA Cup semi-finals on the BBC
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain how you can watch, listen and follow the FA Cup semi-finals.

item.title: Man City interested in Fernandez - Friday's gossip
item.description: Enzo Fernandez is wanted by Manchester City, Aston Villa identify James Trafford as a potential Emiliano Martinez replacement and Manchester United are keen on Micky van de Ven.

item.title: 'Arsenal have been trying to nick 1-0 wins' - do they need mindset change?
item.description: Arsenal and Manchester City are locked on points and goal difference heading into the final five games of the Premier League season.

item.title: Why are McBurnie and Stewart not in World Cup mix?
item.description: Oli McBurnie and Ross Stewart are arguably Scotland's two most in-form strikers, yet neither appear to be in Steve Clarke's World Cup plans, writes Tom English.

item.title: Is this English football's greatest ever escape?
item.description: After 13 straight league losses and with the club 11 points from safety, Gateshead produced a miraculous run to stay in the National League.

item.title: World Cup 2026 injury watch: Key names racing to be ready
item.description: After Spain's Lamine Yamal was ruled out of the rest of his club season, BBC Sport looks at other key names racing to be fit for the World Cup - and those who are set to miss out through injury.

item.title: Leicester 'face £70m black hole' after disastrous football-on-credit-card gamble
item.description: Leicester City have enjoyed the good times, now they are going to have to endure the bad - and there are signs they have not hit rock bottom.

item.title: Why Wolves' relegation had been coming after slow decline
item.description: Wolves have been relegated from the Premier League after just three wins in their first 33 games of the season.

item.title: Respecting the ball, losing the players - inside Rosenior's reign
item.description: Liam Rosenior lasted just 106 days at Chelsea. So what went wrong? And how did it go wrong so quickly?

item.title: Premier League quiz: Name every manager departure this season
item.description: A Premier League club has parted ways with a permanent head coach on 10 occasions this season - can you name them all?

item.title: 50 days until World Cup - pick your favourite kits
item.description: BBC Sport has highlighted some of the most interesting kits which will be seen at this summer's World Cup. Pick your favourites.

item.title: Two down, one to go - who will join Burnley & Wolves?
item.description: With Wolves and Burnley now relegated, BBC Sport looks at which side is most likely to join them in the Championship next season.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: Who can still receive a yellow card suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: Rangers' Rohl wants 'biggest goodbye' for Tavernier
item.description: Danny Rohl hopes captain James Tavernier can bring his Rangers career to a close with a "beautiful moment" by lifting the Scottish Premiership trophy.

item.title: FC Basel postpone fixture after stadium fire
item.description: FC Basel's Swiss Super League match against FC Thun is postponed after a fire causes "significant damage" to Basel's St Jakob-Park stadium.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies five days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: Man City not distracted as WSL title nears - Jeglertz
item.description: Manchester City manager Andree Jeglertz says they are "aware" they can win the Women's Super League title next week - but "it does not affect" their preparation.

item.title: Late Reds boss Beard inducted into WSL Hall of Fame
item.description: Former Liverpool manager Matt Beard is posthumously inducted into the Women's Super League Hall of Fame, along with Casey Stoney and Kerys Harrop.

item.title: Spain defender Batlle close to agreeing Arsenal move
item.description: Spain defender Ona Batlle is close to agreeing a move to Arsenal as a free agent in the summer.

item.title: Chelsea to play all WSL games at Stamford Bridge
item.description: Chelsea say switching all their women's games to Stamford Bridge reaffirms an "ambition and intent" to become "the biggest women's sports club in the world".

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
